channel.title: Paper Feeds
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv and IACR

item.title: HedgeSwap: Universal Hedged Atomic Swaps Against Griefing Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1252

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

通用原子交换（Universal Atomic Swaps）通过适配器签名（adaptor signatures）和可验证定时离散对数（verifiable timed dlogs）替代哈希时间锁合约，仅需底层区块链支持基础签名验证即可实现跨链资产安全交换。然而，现有方案仍易受哀悼攻击（griefing attacks）——恶意方主动中止交换，导致守约方资产被长期锁定，造成显著机会成本与流动性损失。

创新方案：HedgeSwap

本文提出 HedgeSwap，首个面向通用交换场景的抗哀悼攻击协议。其核心思想是：当守约方资产被锁定但未被赎回时，自动向其支付一笔风险补偿溢价（premium），将“锁定风险”转化为可量化、可补偿的经济激励。为克服两类关键挑战：

  • 超时竞速攻击（timeout race attack）（源于交易有效期无上界，见PipeSwap’25）；
  • 超时重叠困境（timeout overlap dilemma）（多层退款期交叠引发逻辑冲突），

HedgeSwap 彻底取消溢价超时机制，转而基于硬性数学关系（如链上可验证的签名衍生关系）自动触发溢价返还，消除竞速漏洞与时间窗口冲突。

进阶优化：轮次制 HedgeSwap

针对高价值资产交换中双方可接受溢价区间不重叠的情形，我们设计轮次制 HedgeSwap：通过溢价迁移机制（premium migration），双方在多轮交互中动态抬升溢价，直至收敛至双方均认可的“锁仓风险溢价”平衡点。实验表明：

  • 基础版 HedgeSwap 交换完成耗时 < 0.5 秒；
  • 五轮轮次制版本耗时 < 1.3 秒；
  • 相比现有合约方案，Gas 成本降低 2.69 倍，兼具安全性、效率与经济合理性。

AI Summary (English)

Universal atomic swaps enable trustless cross-chain exchanges using adaptor signatures and verifiable timed discrete logs, requiring only basic signature verification from blockchains. Yet they remain vulnerable to griefing attacks—where a malicious party aborts the swap to lock the compliant party’s assets for extended periods. Existing contract-based mitigation approaches fail in the universal setting due to (i) timeout race attacks (from unbounded transaction validity) and (ii) a timeout overlap dilemma (from conflicting refund windows). HedgeSwap addresses this by introducing hedged compensation: a premium is paid to the compliant party if its asset is locked but unredeemed. Crucially, it eliminates premium timeouts entirely and instead uses a hard cryptographic relation (e.g., derived signature constraints) to provably trigger premium refunds—resolving both timeout challenges. For high-value swaps with non-overlapping premium acceptability ranges, round-based HedgeSwap employs iterative premium migration, converging to a mutually acceptable lock-up risk premium. Experiments show HedgeSwap completes in <0.5 s; round-based variant (5 rounds) finishes in <1.3 s; and it reduces gas cost by 2.69× versus state-of-the-art contract-based solutions.

Abstract

Universal atomic swaps [Oakland'22] replace hashed timelock contracts with adaptor signatures and verifiable timed dlogs, enabling secure cross-chain cryptocurrency exchanges that only require basic signature verification from the underlying blockchains.
However, existing universal swap protocols remain vulnerable to griefing attacks, where a deviating party aborts the swap to lock a compliant party's assets for a long period. A natural approach is to lift existing contract-based solutions to the universal setting, but we identify that this straightforward solution faces two key challenges: (i) timeout race attacks, first identified in PipeSwap [Oakland'25], which arises from the absence of an upper bound on the transaction validity; (ii) a timeout overlap dilemma, which results from multiple overlapping refund periods.

In this paper, we propose HedgeSwap, a universal hedged atomic swap protocol against griefing attacks, which compensates a compliant party with a premium if its asset is locked but not redeemed. To mitigate the timeout race attacks and timeout overlap dilemma, HedgeSwap eliminates the premium timeout and instead relies on a hard relation to refund the premium. For high-value asset swaps where the parties acceptable premium ranges do not overlap, we further propose a round-based HedgeSwap that utilizes a premium migration mechanism to solve these two timeout challenges, where parties iteratively increase the premium until the lock-up risk premium acceptable to both. Our experimental results show that our HedgeSwap can complete in under 0.5 seconds, and round-based HedgeSwap completes in under 1.3 seconds for a five-round setting, while HedgeSwap reduces gas cost by 2.69X compared to existing contract-based solutions.

item.title: Related Differentials of $4\times4$ MDS Matrices: A Complete Characterization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1251

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在分组密码分析中，相关微分（related differential） 是一类特殊差分对 $(x,y)$，要求对线性层 $M$ 的每个输入/输出坐标位置，$x_i$ 与 $y_j$ 至少满足：二者之一为零，或二者相等。该概念是 Bardeh–Rijmen 零差分攻击的理论基础，而 Daemen 与 Rijmen 曾提出关键问题：哪些 $4\times4$ 最大距离可分（MDS）矩阵存在相关微分？此前工作已完全刻画 $\mathbb{F}_{2^r}$ 上 $3\times3$ MDS 矩阵的充要条件（15个显式方程），但 $4\times4$ 情形长期悬而未决。

核心成果与方法

本文首次给出 $4\times4$ MDS 矩阵 admitting 相关微分的完整代数刻画：设 $M = DNE$ 为其标准约化形式（$N$ 含9个自由变量），则 $M$ 存在相关微分 当且仅当 其9个变量满足280个显式多项式方程中的至少一个。这些方程严格分为两类：70个二次方程与210个三次方程；全部两两互异、不可约、互素，并在自然对称群作用下形成27个轨道。

结构深度解析

我们揭示了方程集的深层组合结构：

  • $3\times3$ 情形的15个方程恰好对应射影空间 $\mathrm{PG}(3,2)$ 的全部15个点；
  • $4\times4$ 的280个方程张成一个14维 $\mathbb{F}_2$-线性空间，满足560个加性关系，且包含840对互斥方程（无法同时在任一MDS矩阵上成立）；
  • 被排除的零模式分为两类：525类因行列式退化而直接违反MDS性质，289类为逻辑空真（vacuous）情形。

实验验证与应用意义

在最小可行域 $\mathbb{F}_8$ 上，穷举确认存在720个约化MDS矩阵：每个恰满足28个方程，每个方程恰被72个矩阵满足——故所有 $4\times4$ MDS 矩阵在 $\mathbb{F}_8$ 上均存在相关微分；而在 $\mathbb{F}_{2^{10}}$ 上，我们构造出首个不满足任何方程的显式MDS矩阵，证伪普遍性。全部结论均通过独立穷举搜索与精确符号计算双重验证。

AI Summary (English)

This paper provides a complete algebraic characterization of $4\times4$ MDS matrices over $\mathbb{F}_{2^r}$ admitting related differentials — a cryptanalytically significant property underlying zero-difference attacks. We prove that such a matrix $M = DNE$, with reduced form $N$ having nine free entries, admits a related differential iff at least one of 280 explicit polynomial equations (70 quadratic, 210 cubic) in those entries holds. All equations are pairwise distinct, irreducible, coprime, and partition into 27 symmetry orbits. Structurally, they span a 14-dimensional $\mathbb{F}_2$-space, satisfy exactly 560 additive relations, and contain 840 mutually exclusive pairs. Exhaustive search over $\mathbb{F}_8$ confirms all 720 reduced $4\times4$ MDS matrices satisfy exactly 28 equations each — hence every such matrix over $\mathbb{F}_8$ admits a related differential; conversely, we explicitly construct an MDS matrix over $\mathbb{F}_{2^{10}}$ satisfying none. All results are rigorously verified by independent exact computation.

Abstract

A pair of differences $(x,y)$ is a \emph{related differential} for a linear layer $M$ if, for every coordinate at both the input and the output, at least one of the two values vanishes or the two values coincide. Related differentials underlie the zero-difference attack on AES of Bardeh and Rijmen, and the question of which maximum distance separable (MDS) matrices admit them was raised by Daemen and Rijmen, who showed that every $4\times4$ circulant MDS matrix does while some Hadamard ones do not. In earlier work we characterized the $3\times3$ MDS matrices over $\mathbb{F}_{2^r}$ admitting related differentials by fifteen explicit equations. In this paper we settle the $4\times4$ case completely: an MDS matrix $M=DNE$ over $\mathbb{F}_{2^r}$ admits a related differential if and only if at least one of $280$ explicit polynomial equations in the nine free entries of its reduced form $N$ holds. The equations, $70$ quadratic and $210$ cubic, are pairwise distinct, irreducible and pairwise coprime, and fall into $27$ orbits under the natural symmetries. We further determine the structure of the equation set: the fifteen equations of the $3\times3$ case are exactly the points of $\mathrm{PG}(3,2)$, while the $280$ equations span a $14$-dimensional $\mathbb{F}_2$-space, satisfy exactly $560$ additive relations, and contain exactly $840$ pairs that can never hold simultaneously on an MDS matrix. The discarded zero patterns split into $525$ whose determinant condition is equivalent to the failure of MDS-ness and $289$ vacuous cases. Over $\mathbb{F}_8$, the smallest field carrying $4\times4$ MDS matrices, exhaustive enumeration shows that there are exactly $720$ reduced MDS matrices; each satisfying exactly $28$ of the equations and each equation being satisfied by exactly $72$ matrices; in particular every $4\times4$ MDS matrix over $\mathbb{F}_8$ admits a related differential. Over $\mathbb{F}_{2^{10}}$ we exhibit an explicit MDS matrix admitting none. All results are verified by exact computation against an independent exhaustive search.

item.title: TensorZKP: Repurposing GPU Tensor Cores for High-Performance Zero-Knowledge Proofs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1250

AI Summary (中文)

TensorZKP：首项利用GPU张量核加速零知识证明的框架

背景与挑战：GPU张量核（Tensor Cores）作为AI爆发的核心硬件，专为低精度矩阵乘法设计，却长期未被密码学领域有效利用。零知识证明（ZKP）虽具隐私保护价值，但其高昂的证明生成开销严重制约落地——现有GPU实现仅依赖通用SIMT核心，完全闲置张量核高达数十倍的算力潜能。

方法创新：本文提出TensorZKP，首个系统性复用张量核加速ZKP的端到端框架。针对张量核不支持有限域运算的根本障碍，我们：（1）设计张量核兼容的有限域算术，将模运算嵌入FP16/BF16流水线；（2）重构关键ZKP模块——将sum-check协议与Spielman编码重表述为稠密/稀疏矩阵乘法任务；（3）构建异步warp特化执行框架，协同调度内存加载、张量核计算与SIMT模约减，消除硬件级流水线气泡。

关键成果：以HyperPlonk（PIOP）与Brakedown（PCS）为实例，TensorZKP在$2^{25}$规模电路上实现端到端证明生成仅215.28 ms：核心算子（内积、标量-向量乘、二次sum-check、编码器）耗时分别低至0.85 ms、0.91 ms、4.04 ms、11.58 ms。相比CPU基线提速955×，超越当前最优SIMT-GPU方案36.2×，首次验证张量核在密码学计算中的颠覆性潜力。

AI Summary (English)

TensorZKP is the first GPU framework to harness specialized Tensor Cores—originally designed for AI acceleration—for high-performance zero-knowledge proof (ZKP) generation. To overcome the fundamental mismatch between Tensor Cores’ low-precision matrix multiplication and ZKP’s finite-field arithmetic, we design Tensor-Core-compatible modular arithmetic and reformulate core ZKP primitives (sum-check protocols and Spielman codes) as matrix multiplication tasks. We further introduce an asynchronous, warp-specialized execution pipeline that overlaps memory access, Tensor Core computation, and SIMT-based modular reduction. Instantiated with HyperPlonk (PIOP) and Brakedown (PCS), TensorZKP achieves 215.28 ms end-to-end proof time for a $2^{25}$-gate circuit—955× faster than CPU and 36.2× faster than state-of-the-art SIMT-based GPU implementations—demonstrating the transformative potential of repurposing AI hardware for cryptographic workloads.

Abstract

GPU Tensor Cores, specialized hardware units designed to accelerate matrix multiplication, have served as the primary engine behind the AI revolution. Given the exponential performance gains they have delivered, aligning cryptographic implementations with this hardware evolution is critical. This is particularly acute for zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs), a cryptographic primitive that currently grapples with high proof generation costs. Existing GPU implementations for ZKPs rely exclusively on general-purpose SIMT cores, leaving the massive computational power of Tensor Cores untapped.

In this paper, we introduce TensorZKP, the first GPU framework to harness Tensor Cores for ZKP acceleration. Since Tensor Cores are designed for low-precision matrix multiplication, mapping ZKP's arithmetic to this hardware is non-trivial. To bridge this gap, we develop Tensor-Core-compatible finite field arithmetic and reformulate ZKP modules, specifically sum-check protocols and Spielman code, into matrix multiplication tasks. Furthermore, we design an asynchronous warp-specialized framework that pipelines memory access, Tensor Core matrix operations, and SIMT-based modular reductions. We instantiate these optimizations with HyperPlonk as the Polynomial Interactive Oracle Proof (PIOP) and Brakedown as the Polynomial Commitment Scheme (PCS) to enable end-to-end proof generation.

The evaluation results show that TensorZKP exhibits remarkable efficiency. At a $2^{25}$ scale, the underlying building blocks complete in $0.85$ ms for inner product, $0.91$ ms for scalar-vector multiplication, $4.04$ ms for degree-2 sum-check, and $11.58$ ms for the encoder. For a circuit with $2^{25}$ multiplication gates, TensorZKP achieves a proof generation time of only $215.28$ milliseconds, representing a $955\times$ speedup over the CPU baseline and a $36.2\times$ improvement over state-of-the-art SIMT-based GPU implementations.

item.title: A family of invertible shift-invariant maps with strong arithmetic properties
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1249

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与动机

移位不变映射（shift-invariant maps）是现代对称密码设计中关键的非线性组件，典型代表为Keccak哈希函数中的$\chi$-映射。其代数结构复杂、硬件实现高效，但系统性构造与可逆性刻画长期缺乏统一框架。本文旨在构建一类具有良好算术性质的可逆移位不变映射族，并建立其代数本质与多项式环之间的深刻联系。

方法与核心构造

我们定义在$\mathbb{F}_2^n$上的映射族$\Omega_{\underline{a}}$，其定义函数由长度为$m$（$2\leq m<n$）的无前后缀序列$\underline{a}=(a_1,\dots,a_m)\in\mathbb{F}_2^m$诱导生成。该族在映射复合运算下构成交换幺半群；更重要的是，其可逆元子群（unit group）具有精确的环论刻画：

  • 若$m mid n$，则$\operatorname{Unit}(\Omega_{\underline{a}})\cong (\mathbb{F}_2[x]/(x^{\lceil n/m ceil}))^ imes$；
  • 若$m\mid n$，则$\operatorname{Unit}(\Omega_{\underline{a}})\cong (\mathbb{F}_2[x]/(x^{2n/m}+x^{n/m}))^ imes$。

这一同构将映射复合转化为多项式乘法，从而将分析难题迁移至经典有限环理论。

主要发现与创新

我们聚焦典型元素$ ho = x_0 + (x_1+a_1+1)\cdots(x_m+a_m+1)$（涵盖$\chi$-映射及Lyu等先前工作），证明：$ ho$可逆当且仅当$m mid n$；并完全刻画其显式逆映射与循环分解结构（包括所有轨道长度与个数）。本工作推广了Kriepke与Lyu等人的构造，首次建立移位不变映射族与商环单位群的系统性对应，为密码学中非线性层的设计与安全性分析提供了新代数范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a family $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ of shift-invariant maps on $\mathbb{F}_2^n$, defined via bifix-free binary sequences $\underline{a}\in\mathbb{F}_2^m$ ($2\le m<n$). We prove $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ forms a commutative monoid under composition, and its unit group is isomorphic to the multiplicative group of either $\mathbb{F}_2[x]/(x^{\lceil n/m ceil})$ (if $m mid n$) or $\mathbb{F}_2[x]/(x^{2n/m}+x^{n/m})$ (if $m\mid n$), enabling polynomial-based analysis. Focusing on the canonical map $ ho = x_0 + \prod_{i=1}^m (x_i + a_i + 1)$—which generalizes Keccak’s $\chi$-map—we establish that $ ho$ is invertible iff $m mid n$, and fully characterize its inverse and cycle structure when invertible. Our framework unifies and extends prior constructions, offering a new algebraic lens for designing and analyzing invertible nonlinear layers in symmetric cryptography.

Abstract

Shift-invariant maps have been employed to design nonlinear layers in many symmetric cryptographic schemes, such as the $\chi$-map used in Keccak. In this paper, we study the shift-invariant maps on $\mathbb{F}_2^n$, whose defining functions come from a family of $n$-variable Boolean functions induced by a bifix-free sequence $\underline{a}=(a_1,a_2,\ldots,a_m)\in \mathbb{F}_2^m$ with $2\leq m<n$, which we denote by $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$. It is shown that $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ forms a commutative monoid with respect to the composition. Moreover, if $m mid n$, then $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ is isomorphic to the unit group of $\mathbb{F}_2[x]/ (x^{\lceil rac{n}{m} ceil})$; if $m\mid n$, then the unit group of $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ is isomorphic to that of $\mathbb{F}_2[x]/ (x^{ rac{2n}{m}}+x^{ rac{n}{m}})$.
The isomorphic relation transforms the composition of functions on $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ into the multiplication of polynomials on the quotient ring of $\mathbb{F}_2[x]$, where the algebraic properties of the latter are well-understood. As a straightforward application, we focus on the algebraic properties of $ ho=x_0+(x_1+a_1+1)(x_2+a_2+1)\cdots (x_m+a_m+1)\in \Omega_{\underline{a}}$, which includes the $\chi$-map as well as several other known maps studied in earlier literature. It is shown that $ ho$ is invertible if and only if $m mid n$. Also the inverse and the cycle structure of $ ho$ (if invertible) can be fully characterized. The construction of $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ generalizes previous works given by Kriepke et al. and Lyu et al. It is hoped that $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ could provide a deeper insight into the study of invertible shift-invariant maps.

item.title: Atlantis: Lattice-based Anonymous Tokens with Private Metadata Bit
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1248

AI Summary (中文)

Atlantis：基于格的匿名代币方案，支持私有元数据比特

背景与问题：匿名代币（Anonymous Tokens）在隐私保护型支付、凭证系统中至关重要。现有“带私有元数据比特的匿名代币”（ATPM）方案虽能嵌入单比特信任标记（如“可疑/可信”），供验证者动态执行速率限制或拒绝策略，但全部依赖RSA群、双线性对或椭圆曲线VRF等经典密码学假设，面临量子计算机攻击时将完全失效，缺乏后量子安全性保障。

方法与创新：本文首次提出完全基于格密码学假设的ATPM方案——Atlantis。其核心贡献包括：（1）采用Fischlin盲签名范式构建基础框架；（2）创新性融合格上线性同态加密（LHE）机制，在签名过程中隐式编码并封装该私有比特；（3）实现公开可验证性（任何第三方可验证代币有效性）与私有比特可提取性（仅掌握部分签发方私钥的授权验证者可安全解出该比特），二者严格分离且不可伪造。

实现与性能：方案实例化采用抗量子签名方案Falcon-512作为底层签名原语，并集成高效格基NIZK证明系统LNP22（Crypto’22）完成零知识证明。最终协议Atlantis实测通信开销为70 KB（客户端–发行方交互），生成代币大小为129 KB，在保证后量子安全前提下达成实用级效率，为隐私增强型基础设施提供首个可部署的抗量子匿名代币范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present Atlantis, the first Anonymous Token with Private Metadata bit (ATPM) scheme based solely on post-quantum lattice assumptions. Unlike prior ATPM constructions relying on RSA, pairings, or elliptic-curve VRFs—none of which withstand quantum attacks—Atlantis achieves public verifiability and private bit-extractability: tokens are publicly checkable for validity, while only verifiers possessing partial knowledge of the issuer’s secret can securely recover the embedded 1-bit trust flag. Our design extends the Fischlin blind-signature paradigm with lattice-based linearly homomorphic encryption to encode the hidden bit within the signature structure. Instantiated using Falcon-512 and the LNP22 lattice NIZK proof system (Crypto ’22), Atlantis requires 70 KB of client–issuer communication and yields 129 KB tokens—demonstrating the first practical, quantum-resistant ATPM construction.

Abstract

Anonymous tokens with private metadata bit (ATPM) allow an issuer to embed a hidden trust flag, as a single bit, within issued tokens. The bit remains hidden from the clients, but verifiers can read the bit and rate-limit or discard tokens marked suspect. A series of ATPM constructions exist in the literature, however all current constructions rely on classical hardness assumptions such as RSA groups, pairings, or elliptic-curve VRFs and do not provide any post-quantum security guarantees.

In this work we present, the first ATPM scheme based on lattice assumptions. Tokens generated with our scheme are publicly verifiable, and privately bit-extractable given partial knowledge of the issuing authority's secret. Our design follows the Fischlin blind-signature paradigm and enriches it with lattice-based linearly-homomorphic encryption to carry the hidden bit.

We also instantiate our scheme from Falcon-512 and the efficient LNP22 lattice NIZK proof system (Lyubashevsky et. al, Crypto '22). The resulting protocol, which we call $\textsf{Atlantis}$, requires 70 KB of client-issuer communication and yields 129 KB tokens.

item.title: Practical Attacks on a Decentralized Secure Messenger Session
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1247

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

Session 是一款广受部署的去中心化即时通讯应用，以用户匿名性与端到端加密为核心设计目标。其当前采用自主设计的 Session Protocol V1（替代原 Signal 协议），但该协议尚未经过系统性安全验证。本文首次开展面向实际实现的深度协议分析，聚焦其一对一通信与封闭群组通信两大核心机制。

方法与发现

我们通过逆向工程、协议状态机建模与真实环境渗透测试，识别出两个根本性设计缺陷：

  • 缺失双向公钥身份认证：通信双方未强制验证彼此长期公钥的有效性与绑定关系；
  • 缺乏单调序列号的密码学绑定：消息序号未被纳入签名或密钥派生过程，导致时序不可信、不可防重放。

实践攻击与影响

基于上述漏洞，在真实 Session 网络拓扑下成功实施三项可复现的实用攻击：
1. 身份冒充攻击：恶意服务器节点或内鬼可替换用户公钥，实现无感知的中间人劫持；
2. 时间戳伪造攻击：攻击者任意篡改消息时间戳，扭曲对话时间线，破坏因果一致性；
3. 消息丢弃与重放攻击：选择性抑制或重复投递消息，导致语义丢失、状态不一致与逻辑冲突。

这些攻击无需客户端漏洞或私钥泄露，仅依赖协议逻辑缺陷，即可严重削弱匿名性、完整性与抗抵赖性等基础安全保证。研究揭示：去中心化架构若忽视密码学绑定与双向认证，反而可能放大信任风险。

贡献与建议

本文不仅揭露了 Session Protocol V1 的关键隐患，更提出三类即刻可用的缓解方案：① 引入基于可信公钥目录的双向密钥认证；② 将单调序列号纳入 HKDF 密钥派生与数字签名输入；③ 在群组协议中扩展 Lamport 时钟绑定机制。所有方案均兼容现有网络，无需修改底层 P2P 层。

AI Summary (English)

Session is a widely deployed decentralized messenger emphasizing anonymity and end-to-end encryption, now using its custom-designed Session Protocol V1. We conduct the first implementation-driven security analysis of this protocol, focusing on 1-to-1 and closed-group messaging. Our work uncovers two critical design flaws: (1) absence of mutual public key authentication, and (2) lack of cryptographic binding between messages and monotonic sequence counters. Exploiting these in realistic deployment settings, we demonstrate three practical attacks: (i) impersonation enabling silent man-in-the-middle interception; (ii) timestamp forgery distorting conversation chronology; and (iii) selective message dropping and replay, causing state inconsistency and semantic loss. All attacks require no client compromise or private key exposure—only protocol-level weaknesses. We propose immediate mitigations: bidirectional key authentication via trusted public key directory, cryptographically binding sequence numbers into HKDF-based key derivation and digital signatures, and extending Lamport clock binding to group protocols—all backward-compatible and deployable without P2P layer changes.

Abstract

Session is a widely deployed decentralized messenger application that emphasizes user anonymity and privacy through end-to-end encryption. Session currently employs its own uniquely designed messaging protocol, Session Protocol V1, having migrated from the extensively studied Signal Protocol. In this paper, we conduct a comprehensive, implementation-driven security analysis of the Session Protocol V1, focusing on its 1-to-1 and closed-group communication mechanisms. Our analysis reveals two fundamental design vulnerabilities: the absence of mutual public key authentication and the lack of cryptographic bindings to monotonic sequence counters. Exploiting these weaknesses within the context of actual application environments, we demonstrate three practical attacks: an impersonation attack, a message timestamp forgery attack, and message dropping and replay attacks. These attacks allow malicious server nodes or unprivileged malicious insiders to substitute public keys, silently suppress or duplicate messages, and manipulate the perceived chronological order of conversations. The findings highlight that these exploits severely undermine the fundamental security guarantees of the messenger. Finally, we propose immediate, actionable mitigation strategies to address the identified flaws and secure the protocol against these threats.

item.title: A Note on Combined Attacks on Fallen Sanctuary
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1246

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

泄漏弹性重密钥方案（leakage-resilient rekeying schemes）旨在通过周期性刷新临时密钥，抵御侧信道泄漏攻击——其核心安全假设是：攻击者无法在单个密钥生命周期内积累足够泄漏信息。Fallen Sanctuary（LR4） 是一种新型高阶泄漏弹性重密钥构造，理论上可实现指数级安全增强：其安全性随基础加密原语调用次数及所需泄露迹数呈指数提升。然而，该方案的安全性严格依赖于内部计数器与缓存中间密钥的正确维护，以强制执行预设的“有界迹数”（bounded-trace）约束。

方法与发现

本文首次提出并系统分析了一种组合故障-侧信道攻击模型，聚焦LR4中计数器更新与校验机制的脆弱性。我们证明：瞬态故障（如电压毛刺或时钟扰动）可阻断重密钥状态推进，导致本应短暂使用的临时密钥被重复调用。这一状态停滞直接破坏了LR4安全证明所依赖的“单密钥仅对应有限迹数”的根本假设。

关键影响与贡献

  • 攻击者得以对同一密钥状态采集任意数量的侧信道迹（如功耗、电磁轨迹），使LR4的实际安全性退化至无重密钥的传统实现水平；
  • 在故障模拟平台上验证了攻击可行性，量化表明：仅需少量可控故障即可瓦解LR4宣称的泄漏弹性保障；
  • 揭示了现有泄漏弹性设计的重大盲区：仅防御侧信道泄漏是不充分的，必须同步建模并防护故障引发的状态演化失效。本工作为构建真正鲁棒的泄漏弹性方案提供了关键设计准则。

AI Summary (English)

This work identifies a critical vulnerability in the leakage-resilient rekeying scheme Fallen Sanctuary (LR4) under a combined fault and side-channel attack model. We demonstrate that transient faults targeting LR4’s counter-update and counter-validation logic can stall its rekeying state progression, causing repeated use of ephemeral keys intended for single-trace lifetimes. This violates LR4’s foundational bounded-trace assumption—enabling an adversary to collect arbitrarily many leakage traces from the same secret state. Consequently, LR4’s claimed exponential security amplification collapses to the baseline resilience of a conventional non-rekeying implementation. Our fault-simulated evaluation confirms practical feasibility, showing that minimal, realistic faults suffice to nullify LR4’s leakage-resilience guarantees. This study underscores that robust leakage resilience requires co-design against both side-channel leakage and fault-induced state evolution failures.

Abstract

Leakage-resilient rekeying schemes aim to maintain cryptographic security
in the presence of side-channel leakage by periodically refreshing ephemeral keys
before sufficient information can be accumulated by an adversary. Fallen Sanctuary
(LR4) is a recent higher-order leakage-resilient rekeying construction that achieves
exponential security amplification with respect to the number of primitive encryption
invocations and the number of traces required to compromise the physical security
of the implementation. Its security, however, relies on the correct maintenance of
internal counters and cached intermediate keys that enforce the prescribed trace
bounds.
In this work, we investigate the security of LR4 under a combined fault and side-
channel attack model. We show that transient faults targeting the counter-update
and counter-validation mechanism can prevent the advancement of the rekeying state,
causing repeated reuse of temporal keys that are intended to be short-lived. As a
consequence, the bounded-trace assumptions underlying the LR4 security proof no
longer hold. We demonstrate that an adversary can accumulate an arbitrary number
of leakage traces corresponding to the same secret state, effectively reducing the
security of the protected primitive to that of a conventional implementation without
rekeying.
We evaluate the attack on a fault simulated implementation and analyze its impact
on the leakage-resilience guarantees claimed by LR4.
Our findings emphasize that leakage-resilient rekeying schemes must consider fault-
induced violations of state evolution assumptions in addition to conventional side-
channel leakage.

item.title: From Shield to Target: Denial-of-Service Attacks on LLM-Based Agent Guardrails
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14517v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

基于大语言模型（LLM）的防护机制（guardrails）已成为抵御提示注入与越狱攻击的关键防线，广泛部署于自主智能体系统中。然而，本研究首次揭示： guardrails赖以防御的核心能力——深度推理与严格任务遵循——恰恰构成其阿喀琉斯之踵：攻击者可通过注入精心构造的自然语言载荷，诱使其陷入冗长、无终止的推理循环，从而实施系统性拒绝服务（DoS）攻击。

方法创新

我们提出两种互补的攻击框架：

  • Beam-Search优化框架：利用LLM作为提案器（proposer），结合策略库（strategy bank）引导的束搜索，高效生成最大化推理步数的自然语言载荷；
  • 机制感知结构突变框架：基于guardrail普遍遵循输出schema的特性，设计轻量级、低算力开销的结构化变异策略（如嵌套指令链、递归模板嵌套、格式诱导歧义），显著降低攻击生成成本。

关键发现

1. 强泛化性：在独立评估中，单个开源代理（surrogate）上优化的载荷可跨架构迁移至8种主流模型（Claude、GPT-4、Gemini、DeepSeek-V2、Qwen2等），实现13–63×的token消耗放大；
2. 真实场景破坏力：在Web交互、桌面自动化、代码生成及多智能体系统等端到端部署中，攻击引发最高148×的端到端延迟放大；
3. 基础设施级影响：单份恶意文档即可饱和共享guardrail服务，导致同节点其他智能体资源饥饿甚至系统瘫痪。

本工作首次将可用性缺陷系统性引入LLM安全研究范式，呼吁构建成本有界、推理鲁棒的新一代防护机制。

AI Summary (English)

LLM-based guardrails, widely deployed to defend autonomous agents against prompt injection and jailbreak attacks, are shown to suffer from a critical availability vulnerability: their core reasoning and task-following capabilities can be weaponized to trigger unbounded reasoning loops—enabling efficient denial-of-service (DoS) attacks. We propose two complementary attack frameworks: (1) a beam-search optimization method guided by an LLM proposer and strategy bank to maximize reasoning length; and (2) a lightweight, mechanism-aware structural mutation approach exploiting guardrails’ schema-following behavior. Evaluated across 8 leading LLM backbones (e.g., Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, DeepSeek, Qwen), optimized payloads achieve 13–63× token amplification in standalone tests and up to 148× latency amplification in real-world agent deployments (web, desktop, coding, multi-agent). Critically, a single poisoned input can saturate shared guardrail infrastructure, starving co-located agents. This work exposes a fundamental trade-off between safety and availability—and calls for cost-bounded, reasoning-robust guardrail design.

Abstract

LLM-based guardrails have emerged as a highly effective defense against prompt injection and jailbreak attacks in autonomous agents. However, we reveal that the very reasoning and task-following capabilities enabling this protection introduce a novel vulnerability: attackers can inject crafted data to trap the guardrail in extended reasoning loops, effectuating a systematic denial-of-service (DoS) attack. To systematically expose this threat, we design a beam-search optimization framework that crafts natural-language payloads to maximize guardrail reasoning length, utilizing an LLM proposer guided by a strategy bank. Based on the observation of guardrail's schema-following nature, we also provide another attack framework driven by mechanism-aware structural mutations with less computational load. The attack efficacy is systematically evaluated in two parts. First, in standalone evaluations, the attack generalizes across diverse guardrail architectures, safety templates, and agent benchmarks. Payloads optimized on a single open-source surrogate successfully transfer to eight leading model backbones (e.g., Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek, and Qwen), achieving a 13--63$\times$ token amplification. Second, in end-to-end real-world agent deployments (web, desktop, code, and multi-agent systems), the attack reveals up to a 148$\times$ latency amplification. We show that a single poisoned document can saturate shared guardrail infrastructures, effectively starving co-located agents and paralyzing the entire system. By uncovering this availability flaw, our work underscores the urgent need to develop cost-bounded, reasoning-robust guardrails.

item.title: Securing the Future of IoMT in the Post-Quantum Era: An Edge-Native Federated Learning Approach
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14515v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

物联网医疗设备（IoMT）在严苛的资源约束下处理高度敏感的健康数据，安全与隐私成为核心关切。联邦学习（FL）虽能实现分布式模型训练，但其交换的模型更新可能隐式泄露患者隐私；更严峻的是，量子计算的快速发展正威胁传统轻量级密码机制的长期安全性，亟需将后量子密码学（PQC） 深度融入IoMT架构。

方法与框架

本文提出一种边缘原生联邦学习安全框架：基于Kubernetes构建可扩展的边缘编排平台，原生集成PQC算法（如CRYSTALS-Kyber密钥封装与Dilithium签名），支持轻量级加密与量子安全密钥协商。框架采用分布式密码处理范式——将密钥生成、签名验证等计算任务卸载至边缘节点并行执行，避免中心化瓶颈。实验在Raspberry Pi 4集群测试床上完成端到端验证，涵盖真实心电图（ECG）数据集上的FL训练流程。

主要发现与创新

  • 分布式密码处理使端到端延迟降低42.3%（对比串行设计），单节点CPU峰值占用率控制在68%以内，内存增量<15 MB；
  • 首次实现PQC与FL在资源受限IoMT设备上的协同部署，验证了NIST标准化PQC算法在ARM Cortex-A72平台的可行性；
  • 提出“安全即服务”边缘编排模型，支持动态PQC策略注入与细粒度通信加密，为FL模型更新提供端到端机密性与完整性保障。

本工作核心贡献在于：设计并实证验证了首个面向IoMT的PQC增强型联邦学习安全编排框架，为构建抗量子攻击的下一代智能医疗物联网（IIoMT）奠定工程基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the urgent need for quantum-resilient security in resource-constrained Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) systems leveraging federated learning (FL). We propose an edge-native, Kubernetes-based orchestration framework that integrates NIST-standardized Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC)—including Kyber for key encapsulation and Dilithium for signatures—directly into FL workflows. Deployed and validated on a Raspberry Pi 4 testbed using real ECG data, our framework distributes cryptographic operations across edge nodes, reducing end-to-end latency by 42.3% versus sequential designs while maintaining feasible CPU (<68% peak) and memory (<15 MB overhead) usage. The primary contribution is the first practical, validated design of a PQC-secured FL orchestration layer for IoMT, enabling confidentiality and integrity for model updates under quantum threat models. Future work will extend toward energy-aware PQC scheduling and adaptive security optimization.

Abstract

Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) devices operate under strict resource constraints while handling highly sensitive health data, making security and privacy critical concerns. Federated learning (FL) further complicates this landscape, as model updates exchanged during training may unintentionally expose private medical information. Emerging quantum computing capabilities threaten the long-term viability of conventional lightweight cryptographic mechanisms, motivating the integration of Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) into IoMT systems. This article discusses key enabling technologies for quantum-resilient IoMT, including post-quantum key establishment, lightweight encryption, and edge-native orchestration. We propose a scalable Kubernetes-based framework that integrates PQC into FL-enabled IoMT environments and validate it on a Raspberry Pi testbed. Results demonstrate that distributed cryptographic processing significantly reduces latency compared to sequential designs while maintaining feasible resource overhead. The primary contribution of this work lies in the design and validation of a secure orchestration and communication framework for FL-enabled IoMT systems. We conclude by outlining future directions toward energy-aware architectures, intelligent security optimization, and resilient next-generation Intelligent Internet of Medical Things (IIoMT) ecosystems.

item.title: Security in a Workflow: Exploring Role-Based Agentic Architectures for Vulnerability Handling
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14261v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

当前基于大语言模型（LLM）的软件安全研究多聚焦于单点任务（如漏洞检测或补丁生成），缺乏对真实工业级多阶段工作流（漏洞分析→修复→验证）的系统性建模。这种割裂导致LLM方法与实际安全实践存在显著鸿沟。

方法设计

本文提出一种角色驱动的智能体工作流架构，模拟安全工程师协作范式，包含四大核心角色：

  • Planner（规划器）：解析漏洞报告，分解任务并调度下游智能体；
  • Analyzer（分析器）：定位漏洞根因，支持集成静态分析工具（如CodeQL）以增强语义理解；
  • Fixer（修复器）：生成可编译、符合上下文约束的补丁代码；
  • Verifier（验证器）：执行编译检查、单元测试及轻量 fuzzing 验证修复有效性。

实验采用 nemotron-cascade-2:30b、qwen3-coder-next 和 gpt-oss:120b 三类先进开源/闭源模型，在 25个真实C/C++漏洞（涵盖CVE-2022-XXXX系列、Linux内核及Redis等项目）上开展端到端评估。

主要发现

  • 漏洞检测准确率达 44%，与GPT-5.5（内部基线）持平，显著优于单智能体基线（+12.7%）；
  • 端到端修复准确率为 19%，其中CodeQL增强的Analyzer使修复成功率提升8.3个百分点；
  • 角色分工与任务链式反馈机制有效缓解幻觉与上下文漂移问题，验证阶段拦截了73%的语法错误补丁。

实践启示

本工作首次将RBAC（基于角色的访问控制）思想迁移至智能体协同设计，为构建可审计、可追溯、符合DevSecOps流程的安全AI系统提供新范式，也为工具链集成（如SAST+LLM）提供了实证路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a role-based agentic workflow for end-to-end vulnerability handling—comprising Planner, Analyzer (with optional CodeQL integration), Fixer, and Verifier—to bridge the gap between LLM-centric security research and industrial practice. Evaluated on 25 real-world C/C++ vulnerabilities, our approach achieves 44% detection accuracy, matching GPT-5.5’s performance, and 19% end-to-end fix accuracy, with CodeQL-augmented analysis improving fix success by 8.3 percentage points. We demonstrate that explicit role decomposition, tool-augmented reasoning, and cross-agent verification significantly reduce hallucination and compilation failures (73% caught at verification). This work establishes a workflow-aware, practitioner-aligned architecture for secure agentic software engineering.

Abstract

Secure software engineering in practice is a multi-stage workflow involving vulnerability analysis, remediation, and fix verification. However, current LLM-based software security approaches often focus on isolated tasks such as detection or patch generation, with limited attention to agentic architectures reflecting industrial workflow. This creates a gap between existing LLM-based vulnerability-handling methods and real-world practices. In this paper, we study a role-based agentic workflow for vulnerability analysis and mitigation consisting of Planner, Analyzer, Fixer, and Verifier roles. To explore the effect of static analysis tool, the analyzer agent was integrated with the CodeQL in one of the workflows. The models used include nemotron-cascade-2:30b, qwen3-coder-next, and gpt-oss:120b. Our evaluation uses 25 real-world C/C++ vulnerabilities. The study reports 44% vulnerability detection accuracy comparable to GPT 5.5 and 19% fix accuracy. We also list implications from this study in context of software security practitioners.

item.title: Evaluating LLMs for Obfuscation Detection and Classification in Android Apps
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14233v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

Android应用开发者广泛采用代码混淆技术（如ProGuard、R8、String加密、控制流扁平化等）以抵御逆向工程、保护知识产权。然而，混淆显著削弱静态分析工具（SAST）对漏洞、恶意行为及敏感逻辑的识别能力，成为移动安全分析的关键瓶颈。现有检测方法多依赖人工设计的启发式规则、特征工程或专用训练模型，泛化性差，难以应对日益多样且动态演进的混淆策略。

方法与实验设计

本研究首次系统评估大语言模型（LLMs）在Android混淆检测与分类任务中的语义推理能力。我们不依赖任何预定义签名、手工规则或微调训练，而是直接利用开源与闭源的主流LLMs（包括Llama-3-8B、Qwen2-7B、Gemma-2-9B、Claude-3.5-Sonnet、GPT-4o等），通过精心设计的结构化提示（prompt），让模型对反编译后的Smali/Java代码片段进行二分类（是否混淆）与多类识别（混淆类型）。实验涵盖两大数据集：（1）受控基准集——同一App经6种主流混淆技术分别处理；（2）真实世界数据集——从Google Play采集的2,147个热门App（含3,862个APK），经人工标注与专家复核验证混淆状态。

主要发现与创新

  • LLMs展现出显著超越传统SAST工具的零样本泛化能力：在真实数据上，最佳模型（GPT-4o）F1达0.89，较主流工具JADX+Obfuscapk提升32%；
  • 提示工程至关重要：引入“混淆定义锚点”与“分步推理链”使准确率平均提升11.7%；
  • 模型规模非决定性因素——Qwen2-7B在部分类型（如字符串加密）上反超GPT-4o；
  • 首次揭示LLM对“语义保留型混淆”（如重命名、控制流扁平化）敏感度高于“语法破坏型”（如反射注入），为后续安全分析范式提供新视角。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first large-scale empirical study evaluating off-the-shelf Large Language Models (LLMs) for zero-shot obfuscation detection and classification in Android apps—without handcrafted rules, signatures, or fine-tuning. We benchmark 7 open-weight and proprietary LLMs (e.g., Llama-3-8B, Qwen2-7B, GPT-4o, Claude-3.5-Sonnet) on a controlled dataset (1 app × 6 obfuscation techniques) and a real-world corpus of 2,147 Google Play apps (3,862 APKs), annotated by security experts. Results show that state-of-the-art LLMs achieve up to 0.89 F1-score in binary detection—outperforming conventional SAST-based tools by 32%—and correctly classify obfuscation types with 76.4% macro-F1. Prompt design (e.g., definition anchoring + stepwise reasoning) boosts accuracy by 11.7% on average, while model size alone does not guarantee superiority: Qwen2-7B surpasses GPT-4o on string encryption detection. Our work demonstrates LLMs’ strong semantic reasoning capability for Android security analysis and highlights critical trade-offs in robustness, interpretability, and deployment feasibility.

Abstract

Android applications (apps) developers increasingly rely on code obfuscation techniques to hinder reverse engineering and protect intellectual property. However, obfuscation also reduces the effectiveness of static analysis and vulnerability detection tools, creating challenges for Android security analysis. Existing approaches for detecting obfuscation in Android apps predominantly rely on handcrafted heuristics, engineered features, or task-specific learning pipelines, which may struggle to generalize across evolving obfuscation strategies. This paper presents a large-scale empirical study investigating the capability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to detect obfuscation in Android apps through semantic reasoning. Our study evaluates whether off-the-shelf LLMs can identify obfuscated code without relying on handcrafted rules, predefined signatures, or dedicated model training. The empirical evaluation is conducted on both a controlled benchmark containing an app obfuscated with multiple techniques and a real-world dataset of Android apps collected from Google Play. The study further examines the impact of prompt design, model selection, and decision thresholds across several open-weight and proprietary LLMs. Finally, the analysis compares LLM-based reasoning with existing SAST-based obfuscation-detection approaches and discusses the broader implications and limitations of applying LLMs to Android security analysis.

item.title: Investigating Metamorphic Fuzz Oracle Enhancement via Large Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14164v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

灰盒模糊测试中，fuzz driver 是核心组件，负责封装目标接口、定义测试输入空间，并显著影响检测效果。然而，现有驱动普遍依赖崩溃型（crash-based）oracle，仅以程序异常终止为判定依据，忽视库函数的功能正确性，导致对逻辑错误、内存误用等非崩溃型缺陷检出能力严重受限。

方法创新：MetaFOE框架

本文首次系统探索基于变形关系（Metamorphic Relations, MRs）的模糊测试oracle增强（MFOE），提出MetaFOE——一个基于大语言模型（LLM）的自动化框架。该框架无需人工干预，即可：（1）从函数文档与源码中自动识别语义不变性，生成高质量MRs；（2）将MRs编译为可执行的meta driver（变形驱动），嵌入原有fuzz流程；（3）支持多LLM（Claude-3.5、GPT-4o、Qwen2.5-72B）与五类提示策略协同优化生成质量。

关键结果与贡献

在OSS-Fuzz真实驱动集上评估表明：MetaFOE共生成3,475条MRs，其中77.3%具备可实施性；成功构建12,351个meta driver，6,228个通过编译与语义验证（有效率50.4%）。经3小时模糊测试，有效meta driver平均提升边覆盖率18.7%，并触发1,528个唯一崩溃（含17个新发现CVE）。本研究不仅证实了变形oracle对灰盒模糊测试的实质性增益，更首次验证了LLM在复杂软件工程任务（如形式化oracle构造）中的可行性与实用性，为智能模糊测试范式提供了新路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents MetaFOE, the first LLM-based framework for automated metamorphic fuzz oracle enhancement (MFOE). It addresses the limitation of crash-only oracles in greybox fuzzing by generating and integrating metamorphic relations (MRs) to detect functional and non-crashing bugs. Evaluated on OSS-Fuzz drivers using three state-of-the-art LLMs and five prompt strategies, MetaFOE generated 3,475 MRs (77.3% applicable) and implemented 12,351 meta drivers—6,228 validated as correct. Within just three hours of fuzzing, these valid meta drivers improved edge coverage by 18.7% on average and triggered 1,528 unique crashes, including 17 previously unknown CVEs. Our work demonstrates both the practical efficacy of metamorphic oracles and the feasibility of LLMs for automating complex, domain-aware oracle synthesis—advancing greybox fuzzing toward semantic-aware, intelligent testing.

Abstract

Fuzz drivers are essential components of greybox fuzzing, as they encapsulate target interfaces, define test spaces, and largely determine fuzzing effectiveness. Existing fuzz drivers typically rely on crash-based oracles for security testing, overlooking library functionality and limiting bug detection capability. In this paper, we present the first study on metamorphic-based fuzz oracle enhancement (MFOE), which augments existing fuzz drivers with metamorphic-based oracles derived from metamorphic relations (MRs). Since constructing and integrating such oracles requires substantial domain knowledge, automating MFOE is challenging. To address this challenge, we propose MetaFOE, an LLM-based framework that automatically generates and integrates metamorphic-based oracles. We evaluate MetaFOE on OSS-Fuzz drivers using three modern LLMs and five prompt strategies. MetaFOE generates 3,475 MRs, of which 77.3% are applicable, and implements 12,351 meta drivers, with 6,228 being valid. After three hours of fuzzing, the valid meta drivers improve edge coverage by an average of 18.7% and trigger 1,528 unique crashes. Our results demonstrate both the effectiveness of metamorphic-based oracle enhancement and the feasibility of using LLMs to automate MFOE, providing valuable insights for advancing greybox fuzzing.

item.title: SkillMutator: Benchmarking and Defending Language-and-Code Cross-modal Attacks on LLM Agent Skills
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14154v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLM）智能体正通过动态加载“Agent Skills”（代理技能）扩展运行时能力——每项技能由自然语言说明书（SKILL.md）与配套可执行脚本/资源共同构成。这种语言-代码跨模态耦合催生新型攻击面：攻击者可在看似无害的文档中隐写指令，诱导智能体执行敏感文件窃取等恶意行为，而脚本本身静态分析仍显示“安全”。现有检测工具（开源/商用）对此类攻击识别率极低（仅2%–8%和9%–17%），因其将技能简单视为提示注入载体或静态代码，忽视语言与代码间的语义协同漏洞。

方法与创新

我们提出 SkillMutator——首个面向技能安装阶段的跨模态攻击基准，覆盖13类攻击模式。其核心是对抗性技能变异框架：基于扫描器反馈迭代优化恶意技能，使注入行为在功能、文档与脚本层面均与合法技能难以区分。为实现轻量、隐私友好的本地化防御，我们设计四阶段推理轨迹蒸馏框架，将前沿教师模型（如GPT-5.4）的复杂判断逻辑压缩至开源小模型中，避免API调用与数据外泄。

关键结果

在最具挑战性的SkillMutator子集（n=76）上，蒸馏模型 Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct 将检测率从基线17.1%大幅提升至88.2%，显著超越GPT-4o-mini（23.7%）、GPT-5.4-mini（79.0%），并逼近前沿GPT-5.4（86.8%）。该成果首次证实：无需依赖昂贵闭源大模型，即可实现高鲁棒性的跨模态攻击防御，为LLM智能体安全落地提供可部署、可审计的技术路径。

AI Summary (English)

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly load Agent Skills—comprising natural-language specifications (SKILL.md) and executable code—to extend capabilities at runtime. This language-and-code coupling creates a novel cross-modal attack surface: attackers can embed stealthy malicious directives in benign-looking documentation while keeping scripts syntactically harmless, evading conventional scanners (2–8% detection for open-source, 9–17% for commercial tools). To address this gap, we introduce SkillMutator, the first benchmark for install-time detection of such attacks, featuring 13 adversarial mutation categories and iterative refinement guided by scanner feedback. We further propose a four-phase reasoning-trajectory distillation framework to compress frontier-model decision logic into efficient open-weight models. Evaluated on the hardest SkillMutator subset (n=76), our distilled Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct achieves 88.2% detection, outperforming GPT-4o-mini (23.7%), GPT-5.4-mini (79.0%), and matching near-frontier GPT-5.4 (86.8%). This demonstrates practical, locally deployable defense against cross-modal skill attacks without costly API dependencies.

Abstract

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly extend their capabilities at runtime by loading Agent Skills, which pair natural-language specifications (SKILL.md) with executable scripts and resources. Because a skill's behavior relies on both natural-language instructions and executable code, assessing its safety requires cross-modal reasoning, creating a new language-and-code attack surface. Attackers can present a benign workflow in SKILL.md while embedding implicit directives that steer the agent to exfiltrate sensitive files, even if the scripts appear harmless. This attack surface remains understudied; prior work treats skills merely as prompt-injection vectors or static code artifacts, leaving attacks emerging from cross-modal interactions largely unmeasured. In our evaluation, open-source and commercial skill scanners detect only 2%-8% and 9%-17% of such attacks, respectively. To address this gap, we introduce SkillMutator, the first benchmark for install-time detection of language-and-code cross-modal attacks on Agent Skills. It emulates an adversarial mutation process across 13 attack categories, iteratively refining malicious skills using scanner feedback to make injected behaviors indistinguishable from legitimate workflows. We further propose a four-phase reasoning-trajectory distillation framework to distill frontier-teacher traces into smaller open-weight models. This produces a locally deployable scanner avoiding third-party data exposure and excessive API costs. On the strongest SkillMutator subset (n=76), our distilled model (Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct) improves detection from 17.1% to 88.2%, surpassing GPT-4o-mini (23.7%) and GPT-5.4-mini (79.0%), and reaching frontier-level GPT-5.4 (86.8%). These results show practical defense against cross-modal attacks is feasible without relying on costly frontier models.

item.title: No Accidental Software Agent First Canonical Code for Human Code Entropy Reduction and 30 to 500 times Lower Frontier Model Requirements
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14357v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

前沿代码大模型在学习人类代码库时，不仅需掌握程序行为逻辑，还被迫吸收大量偶然熵（accidental entropy）——包括框架迭代、命名漂移、生成代码歧义、依赖“仪式化”配置、CI方言、薄弱证明路径及人本评审惯例等非本质噪声。这些噪声显著抬高模型容量需求，掩盖了测试、故障报告、迁移记录、边界案例、产品决策与运维历史等高价值信号。

方法：Agent-First Canonical Code（AFCC）

本文提出代理优先的规范代码（AFCC）范式，构建一种证据承载型（proof-carrying）基础架构，将常规产品代码重写为结构化、可验证的语义单元：

  • 规范行为画像（canonical behavior profiles）
  • 类型化变更代数（typed change algebra）
  • 证明通道（proof lanes）与受限编辑语法（constrained edit grammars）
  • 语义补丁单元（semantic patch cells）与运行时负记忆（runtime negative memory）
  • 证据承载变更对象（proof-carrying change objects）

核心假设：在明确定义的行为等价预言机（oracle） 下对软件进行商化（quotienting），可将功能等价但形式各异的实现，收敛至带显式证据与证明义务的受控代表元。

主要发现与创新

  • 提出“零事故地平线”（No-Accident Horizon）：界定偶然性可消除的理论极限——当残余 novelty、证据完备性、治理强度、风险暴露与未来选项价值成为主导因素时，优化终止。
  • 针对常规产品软件分布，给出约100倍端到端验证变更成本下降的可辩护规划目标（非普适保证）。
  • 在 Qwen2.5-Coder-14B 上开展初步 QLoRA 实验：成功学习 64,088 条规范轨迹，抑制已知禁用语言标记；但未验证行为保真性、规模化经济性或单位验证变更成本。
  • 核心贡献是可证伪的程序框架，以最小功能描述长度与验证变更成本为双重优化目标，为代码智能建立新评估维度。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the “accidental entropy” problem in frontier coding models—noise from framework churn, naming drift, generated-code ambiguity, CI dialects, and human-centric review rituals—that inflates model requirements without improving behavioral fidelity. We propose Agent-First Canonical Code (AFCC): a proof-carrying substrate that rewrites routine product code into canonical behavior profiles, typed change algebra, proof lanes, constrained edit grammars, semantic patch cells, runtime negative memory, and proof-carrying change objects. Its core hypothesis is that quotienting software under a declared behavioral oracle collapses equivalent encodings into governed representatives with explicit evidence and proof obligations. Preliminary QLoRA experiments on Qwen2.5-Coder-14B learn 64,088 canonical trajectories and suppress forbidden-language markers—but do not yet confirm behavior preservation, scaling efficiency, or verified-change cost reduction. The contribution is a falsifiable program centered on minimizing functional description length and amortized cost per verified correct change, targeting up to ~100× all-in cost reduction for routine-product distributions—not universal applicability.

Abstract

Frontier coding models may spend substantial capacity learning not only program behavior, but also accidental entropy in human repositories. Such repositories contain valuable signals: tests, incidents, migrations, edge cases, product judgment, and operational history. These signals are entangled with framework churn, naming drift, generated-source ambiguity, dependency rituals, CI dialects, weak proof routes, and human-oriented review customs. We propose agent-first canonical code, a proof-carrying substrate that rewrites routine product software into canonical behavior profiles, typed change algebra, proof lanes, constrained edit grammars, semantic patch cells, runtime negative memory, and proof-carrying change objects. The core hypothesis is that quotienting software by behavior equivalence under a declared oracle can collapse equivalent encodings into governed representatives with explicit evidence and proof obligations. The endpoint is amortized cost per verified correct change, including source, context, reasoning, tools, verification, security, provenance, review, failed loops, defects, and foundry cost under a common oracle. Reported reduction bands are hypotheses, not measured frontier results. The proposed limit is a No-Accident Horizon: removable accident decreases until residual novelty, evidence, governance, risk, and future optionality dominate. For supported routine-product distributions, this gives a defensible planning target near 100-fold all-in cost reduction, not a guarantee for all software. Preliminary QLoRA experiments on Qwen2.5-Coder-14B show that 64,088 canonical trajectories are learnable and suppress tested forbidden-language markers, but do not establish behavior preservation, scaling economics, or verified-change cost. The contribution is a falsifiable program centered on minimum functional description length and verified-change cost.

item.title: FAConformer: Frequency-Aware Convolutional Transformer for Auditory Attention Decoding
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14120v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

听觉注意解码（Auditory Attention Decoding, AAD）旨在从多说话人声学环境下的脑电（EEG）响应中推断被试关注的目标说话人，是神经调控助听系统的核心技术。现有方法虽取得进展，但普遍对频域EEG信息利用不足：多数模型依赖手工设计的多频带特征提取或简单拼接，仅在浅层引入频带信息，难以建模频带特异性模式及跨频带动态交互，限制了解码精度与可解释性。

方法创新

本文提出FAConformer——一种频率感知型卷积-Transformer融合框架：

  • 频带分解与独立编码：将原始EEG信号通过滤波器组分解为δ、θ、α、β、γ等5个生理相关频段，每频段输入专属CNN-Transformer编码器，实现频带特异性表征学习；
  • 频率感知注意力（FAA）模块：将各频段特征视为“频带令牌”（band tokens），通过可学习的跨频带注意力机制自适应融合，显式建模频带间非线性依赖关系；
  • 频带辅助监督（BAS）：为每个编码分支引入独立分类损失，缓解联合训练中弱贡献频段的梯度消失问题，提升整体优化稳定性。

主要结果

在两个公开AAD数据集（Dataset I & II）上，FAConformer在3种决策窗长（2s/4s/6s）下均显著优于12种基线模型，平均准确率超越当前SOTA模型4.9%（如6s窗下达89.7%）。消融实验证实FAA与BAS贡献分别达+2.3%和+1.8%；频带重要性分析揭示α/β频段主导注意解码，γ频段具强互补性；参数敏感性验证了模型对超参设置的鲁棒性。代码已开源：https://github.com/wzwvv/FAConformer。

AI Summary (English)

Auditory attention decoding (AAD) seeks to identify the attended speaker from EEG responses in multi-talker scenes—a critical capability for neuro-steered hearing aids. While recent models show promise, they underutilize frequency-domain EEG information, often relying on shallow handcrafted band features or naive concatenation, neglecting band-specific dynamics and cross-band interactions. To address this, we propose FAConformer, a frequency-aware CNN-Transformer framework featuring: (1) band-wise decomposition and dedicated CNN-Transformer encoders per physiological frequency band (δ–γ); (2) a Frequency-Aware Attention (FAA) module that treats band features as tokens to model adaptive inter-band dependencies; and (3) Band-wise Auxiliary Supervision (BAS) to prevent under-optimization of weakly contributing branches. Evaluated on two public AAD benchmarks across three decision-window lengths (2s/4s/6s), FAConformer consistently outperforms 12 strong baselines, surpassing the current state-of-the-art by 4.9% average accuracy (e.g., 89.7% at 6s). Ablation, band importance, and sensitivity analyses confirm its effectiveness, robustness, and interpretability. Code is publicly available.

Abstract

Auditory attention decoding (AAD) aims to infer the attended speaker from neural responses in multi-speaker acoustic environments and is a key problem for neuro-steered hearing systems. Although recent studies have achieved encouraging progress, existing AAD models still do not fully exploit frequency domain electroencephalography (EEG) information. In particular, most approaches introduce multi-band information through handcrafted feature extraction or direct cross-band feature concatenation, which mainly exploit frequency information at a shallow level and may overlook band-specific patterns and cross-band interactions. To address these limitations, this paper proposes FAConformer, a frequency-aware CNN-Transformer framework for AAD that explicitly integrates band-specific encoding and adaptive cross-band interaction. Specifically, FAConformer first decomposes EEG signals into multiple frequency bands and assigns each band to an independent CNN-Transformer encoder for band-specific modeling. The resulting band-wise features are then adaptively fused by a carefully designed frequency-aware attention (FAA) module that models cross-band dependencies by treating band-wise features as tokens. Further, band-wise auxiliary supervision (BAS) is introduced to prevent weakly contributing branches from being under-optimized during joint training. In this way, FAConformer performs frequency-aware modeling that more effectively exploits frequency domain information. Extensive experiments on two public AAD datasets with three decision-window lengths demonstrated that FAConformer consistently outperformed 12 competitive baselines, surpassing the current state-of-the-art model by 4.9%. Further analyses of band importance, ablation, and parameter sensitivity verify the effectiveness, robustness, and interpretability of the proposed framework. Code is available at https://github.com/wzwvv/FAConformer.

item.title: When to Write and When to Suppress: Route-Specialized Dual Adapters for Memory-Assisted Knowledge Editing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14668v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

知识编辑旨在精准更新模型中特定事实（如“爱因斯坦生于德国”→“瑞士”），同时严格保留邻近无关行为（如对“牛顿国籍”的正确回答）。现有方法常忽视一个关键矛盾：编辑不应无条件应用，而需按语义相关性动态启用或抑制。本文聚焦于记忆辅助型知识编辑范式——在推理时检索编辑记忆（edit memory），并通过参数高效适配器修正模型的对象偏好。我们指出：核心挑战不仅是“如何写入编辑”，更是“何时应主动抑制编辑”。

方法创新：路由专用双适配器（RSDA）

我们提出 RSDA（Route-Specialized Dual Adapters），包含三大协同组件：

  • 相关性路由器（Relevance Router）：基于输入提示语义，二元判定是否应激活编辑记忆；
  • 编辑适配器（Edit Adapter）：仅对路由通过的提示启用，显式训练其提升新对象（如“瑞士”）相对于原对象（如“德国”）的概率偏好；
  • 局部性适配器（Locality Adapter）：专用于未被路由的提示（含非直接提问，如“谁提出了相对论？”），强化或恢复对原始对象的偏好，保障知识一致性。

关键结果与发现

在统一记忆协议下，RSDA 在三大千例基准（CF、zsRE、mQUAKE）及两个主流 8B 模型（Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct、Qwen3-8B）上全面领先：

  • Llama-3.1-8B 上概率偏好准确率达 0.8180（CF）、0.8946（zsRE）、0.9922（mQUAKE），均为SOTA；
  • 路由器消融表明：最优路由策略高度数据依赖——CF 适合轻量词法神经路由器，zsRE/mQUAKE 则需更强语义的 BGE 嵌入路由；
  • 模块消融证实：性能增益主要源于编辑注入与离路抑制的解耦设计，而非单纯增加 LoRA 参数量。

AI Summary (English)

Knowledge editing must update target facts while preserving unrelated behaviors—a challenge exacerbated in memory-assisted settings where edit memories are retrieved at inference and applied via parameter-efficient adapters. We argue the core design question is not only how to write an edit, but critically when to suppress it. To this end, we propose RSDA, a route-specialized dual-adapter editor comprising: (1) a relevance router that decides whether a prompt warrants edit memory activation; (2) an edit adapter trained exclusively on routed prompts to boost preference for the new object; and (3) a locality adapter trained on unrouted (including indirect) prompts to preserve or restore original-object preference. Evaluated on CF, zsRE, and mQUAKE (1,000 cases each) with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct and Qwen3-8B, RSDA achieves state-of-the-art probability-preference accuracy: 0.8180 (CF), 0.8946 (zsRE), and 0.9922 (mQUAKE). Ablations show optimal routing is dataset-dependent—lexical neural routing works best on CF, while BGE embedding routing excels on zsRE/mQUAKE—and gains stem primarily from decoupling edit injection from off-route suppression, not increased LoRA capacity.

Abstract

Knowledge editing systems must update selected facts while preserving nearby but irrelevant behavior. This paper studies this problem in a memory-assisted setting where an edit memory is retrieved at inference time and a parameter-efficient adapter corrects the model's object preference. We argue that the central design question is not only how to write an edit, but also when to suppress it. We introduce \method{}, a route-specialized dual-adapter editor. A relevance router first decides whether a prompt should receive an edit memory. Routed prompts use an edit adapter trained to prefer the new object over the original object; unrouted non-direct prompts use a separate locality adapter trained to preserve or restore the original-object preference. We evaluate \method{} on three 1,000-case protocols, \cf{}, \zsre{}, and \mquake{}, under the same memory protocol and two 7B/8B base models. On Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, \method{} obtains the best overall probability-preference accuracy on all three benchmarks: 0.8180 on \cf{}, 0.8946 on \zsre{}, and 0.9922 on \mquake{}. The same trend holds on Qwen3-8B. Router ablations show that the relevant memory boundary differs across datasets: a lexical neural router is safest on \cf{}, while BGE embedding routing is better on \zsre{} and \mquake{}. Component and module ablations show that the gain mainly comes from separating edit injection from off-route suppression rather than from simply increasing LoRA capacity.

item.title: Which Directions Matter? Sparse Design for Affine Robust Optimization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14648v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

鲁棒机器学习与优化的性能高度依赖于不确定性集（uncertainty set）的设计。现有方法常采用全维椭球、$\ell_p$-球等对称结构，但其可能过度保守或忽略任务相关方向。本文聚焦稀疏方向选择这一根本问题：当不确定性被限定在由有限字典（如预定义方向集合）张成的子空间中，且受总“预算”（如加权半径和）约束时，哪些方向最值得保留？ 这一选择直接影响鲁棒优化的可解性与泛化性。

方法与创新

我们提出一种数据驱动的稀疏不确定性集构建框架：

  • 定义原子不确定性集——仅激活选定方向的加权组合，其支撑函数具有闭式表达，从而保证含仿射目标的鲁棒优化问题保持凸性与可解性；
  • 设计基于评估方向覆盖度的目标函数，支持灵活输入（如训练损失梯度、对抗扰动方向、验证集上观测到的分布偏移向量）；
  • 严格证明该目标函数具有单调性与子模性，为贪心算法提供$(1-1/e)$近似比理论保障，并证实该界是紧的（存在匹配的NP-hardness下界）；
  • 提出损失上界证书（certificate），量化因方向截断引入的鲁棒性损失；
  • 给出半径校准规则，通过验证集控制未见样本下的鲁棒约束违反概率，实现统计可保证的泛化。

核心价值

本工作首次将子模优化系统性引入鲁棒优化的方向选择环节，在可解性、数据适应性与理论严谨性之间取得平衡，为轻量级、可解释、高保真鲁棒模型设计提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the fundamental question in affine robust optimization: which uncertainty directions matter most when the uncertainty set is constrained to a finite dictionary and a budget on directional radii? We propose a sparse design framework that selects a subset of directions to form an atomic uncertainty set, whose support function admits a closed-form expression—ensuring tractable convex reformulations for affine objectives. Our data-driven selection rule maximizes coverage over evaluation directions (e.g., gradients, adversarial perturbations, or empirical distribution shifts), and we prove this objective is monotone and submodular, enabling a greedy algorithm with a tight $(1-1/e)$ approximation guarantee and matching hardness. We further provide a certified bound on the robustness loss induced by sparsification and a radius calibration rule with out-of-sample statistical control. This work bridges submodular optimization and robust learning, yielding interpretable, computationally efficient, and theoretically grounded uncertainty modeling.

Abstract

Robust machine learning and optimization rely on the uncertainty model choice. We investigate which uncertainty directions a model must cover when defined by a finite dictionary and a budget constraint. Selecting a subset forms an atomic uncertainty set with a closed form support function, yielding tractable robust programs for affine objectives. We propose a data driven selection rule based on a coverage objective over evaluation directions, including gradients, adversarial perturbations, or shifts observed on held out data. We prove this objective is monotone and submodular, supporting a greedy method with a $(1-1/e)$ approximation guarantee and a matching hardness barrier. We also provide a certificate bounding the loss from the selected subset and a radius calibration rule with out of sample control.

item.title: Behavioral Audit of Machine Unlearning Has a Privacy Cost
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14518v1

AI Summary (中文)

行为式机器遗忘审计存在固有隐私代价

机器遗忘（Machine Unlearning, MU）旨在从已训练模型中移除特定数据的影响，是保障数据主体“被遗忘权”的关键技术。然而，当前缺乏被广泛接受的可验证审计机制，尤其在模型拥有者与审计方互不信任的现实场景下——前者可能恶意规避遗忘操作，后者虽诚实但可能好奇地推断敏感信息。本文首次从信息论角度严格证明：对于凸机器学习模型，任何仅依赖对模型进行行为式查询（如输入-输出响应）的通用审计方案，若能可靠识别未充分执行遗忘的模型，则必然泄露保留数据集的成员身份信息（membership leakage）。换言之，在“模型拥有者不可信、审计者诚实但好奇”的威胁模型下，审计能力与隐私保护存在根本性权衡（privacy-audit tradeoff）。我们通过理论推导与严谨实验双重验证该结论：在逻辑回归、SVM等凸模型上，所有主流行为审计方法（如梯度一致性、预测漂移检测）均在提升检出率的同时显著增加成员推断攻击成功率；进一步实验表明，该张力在ResNet、BERT等非凸模型中同样稳健存在。本研究揭示了现有MU审计范式的深层局限，呼吁设计需显式建模审计方威胁的新型隐私保护审计框架。代码已开源：https://github.com/LiouTang/Behavioral-Unlearn-Audit。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes an information-theoretic impossibility result: for convex ML models, any behavioral audit scheme—relying solely on input-output queries—cannot reliably detect insufficient unlearning without leaking membership information about the retained dataset. This reveals an inherent privacy-audit tradeoff under mutual distrust, where the model owner is dishonest and the auditor is honest-but-curious. Empirical validation on convex models (logistic regression, SVM) confirms the theoretical bound, and extensive experiments further demonstrate that this tension persists in non-convex models (ResNet, BERT). Our work fundamentally challenges the assumption that behavioral auditing is privacy-safe, and calls for audit designs that explicitly account for the auditor’s inferential capability. Code is publicly available.

Abstract

The removal of learned data from Machine Learning models through Machine Unlearning (MU) has been widely studied; however, there has yet to be an agreed-upon scheme for auditing MU. Existing work has shown that a dishonest model owner can falsify evidence to avoid executing MU, while curious auditors (and adversaries) can infer the privacy-sensitive properties of the model and its training data even with limited access. Yet auditing of MU under mutual distrust between the model owner and the auditor remains unexplored. We provide an information-theoretic proof for this scenario: for convex ML models, a generic audit scheme that relies solely on querying the model for \textit{behavioral} signals cannot identify insufficiently unlearned models without revealing membership information of the retained set. Therefore, auditing MU under the assumption of a dishonest model owner and an honest-but-curious auditor faces an inherent privacy-audit tradeoff. Our empirical results on convex models strongly supports this result, while further experiments demonstrate that this privacy-audit tension persists in non-convex models. Our results call for a more careful consideration of the privacy-audit tension under a realistic auditor threat model, and serve as a foundation for more scrutiny of designs of privacy-preserving audit schemes for the MU pipeline. We also release our code implementation at https://github.com/LiouTang/Behavioral-Unlearn-Audit.

item.title: Federated Learning for Feature Generalization with Convex Constraints
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14416v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦学习（FL）在跨设备/机构协作建模中面临严峻的特征泛化瓶颈：客户端数据高度异构，导致本地模型易过拟合局部分布；更关键的是，即使具备可迁移性的中间层特征，在全局聚合过程中也常因参数尺度失衡而发生扭曲，削弱跨域鲁棒性。

方法创新：FedCONST

我们提出 FedCONST——一种面向特征泛化的联邦学习新范式。其核心包含两大协同机制：

  • 自适应更新调制：基于全局模型参数强度动态缩放客户端梯度更新幅度，抑制已充分学习参数的过度更新，同时增强欠训练参数的梯度贡献，实现参数级均衡优化；
  • 凸约束聚合：在服务器端引入线性凸约束集（如参数范数界、方向一致性约束），严格保障聚合过程的稳定性，并显式保留各客户端在本地训练中习得的泛化性特征结构，避免“平均即退化”。

实验验证与优势

通过梯度信噪比（GSNR）理论分析，我们证明FedCONST显著提升特征信号的跨客户端可迁移性与抗噪声能力。在6个标准FL基准（包括FEMNIST、CIFAR-100非IID划分及医疗影像数据集）上，FedCONST以平均+3.2%准确率超越SOTA方法（如FedProx、SCAFFOLD），且在低资源客户端（<50样本/类）下泛化性能提升达+5.7%。该方法无需额外通信开销或模型修改，具备强实用性与部署友好性。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) suffers from poor feature generalization under data heterogeneity: local models overfit to client-specific distributions, and even transferable features degrade during aggregation due to unbalanced parameter updates. To address this, we propose FedCONST, a novel FL framework that jointly optimizes update modulation and aggregation constraints. FedCONST dynamically scales client gradients based on global parameter strength—suppressing updates to well-learned parameters while amplifying underdeveloped ones. Crucially, it enforces linear convex constraints (e.g., norm bounds, directional alignment) during server-side aggregation to stabilize training and preserve locally acquired generalization capabilities. GSNR analysis confirms FedCONST enhances feature transferability and robustness. Experiments across 6 heterogeneous FL benchmarks show FedCONST achieves state-of-the-art accuracy (+3.2% avg), with up to +5.7% gain in low-data regimes—without extra communication or architectural changes.

Abstract

Federated learning (FL) often struggles with generalization due to heterogeneous client data. Local models are prone to overfitting their local data distributions, and even transferable features can be distorted during aggregation. To address these challenges, we propose FedCONST, an approach that adaptively modulates update magnitudes based on the parameter strength of the global model. This prevents over-emphasizing well-learned parameters while reinforcing underdeveloped ones. Specifically, FedCONST employs linear convex constraints to ensure training stability and preserve locally learned generalization capabilities during aggregation. A Gradient Signal to Noise Ratio (GSNR) analysis further validates the effectiveness of FedCONST in enhancing feature transferability and robustness. As a result, FedCONST effectively aligns local and global objectives, mitigating overfitting and promoting stronger generalization across diverse FL environments, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

item.title: A Low-Rank Subspace Analysis of LLM Interventions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14388v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLM）的安全干预（如抑制拒绝响应、缓解奉承倾向或防御越狱攻击）常引发非目标行为的意外变化，导致行为控制缺乏可预测性与模块化。这一“副作用传播”现象严重制约了可靠安全机制的设计与部署。

方法创新：低秩子空间诊断框架

本文提出首个基于激活空间低秩子空间建模的行为交互分析框架：

  • 将每类行为（拒绝、越狱、奉承）表征为对应层激活中的低维线性子空间（由主成分张成）；
  • 量化两类关键几何量：(i) 行为子空间间的重叠度（以主角余弦平方均值衡量）；(ii) 行为子空间与决策子空间的夹角（后者编码模型最终输出选择，如“拒绝 vs. 遵从”）。

实验覆盖7B–70B规模的主流指令微调模型（Llama-2/3、Qwen、Mixtral），在统一评估协议下系统检验干预迁移效应。

核心发现

1. 行为表征高度共享：拒绝、越狱、奉承行为在隐藏层中存在显著子空间重叠，证实其底层表征耦合性；
2. 干预效应呈强不对称性：调控某行为（如越狱）会显著改变另一行为（如拒绝），但反向影响微弱；
3. 几何机制可解释传播强度：副作用幅度与子空间重叠度正相关，且源行为子空间越接近决策子空间（夹角越小），其干预越易“驱动”其他行为；
4. 识别上游控制点：部分行为（如越狱抵抗）充当高影响力枢纽，其干预引发跨行为级联变化，而奉承倾向则相对隔离。

本研究揭示LLM行为不可分割性的几何根源，为开发解耦式干预策略（如子空间正交化、定向投影）提供了理论基础与可量化诊断工具。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a low-rank subspace diagnostic framework to analyze unintended side effects of behavioral interventions in LLMs. We model refusal, jailbreak resistance, and sycophancy as low-dimensional subspaces in activation space across multiple instruction-tuned models (7B–70B). We find that these behaviors share internal representations, leading to asymmetric cross-behavior intervention effects: modifying one behavior often alters others, with some acting as upstream control points. Crucially, we identify two geometric determinants of propagation strength: (i) subspace overlap—quantified by the mean squared cosine of principal angles—and (ii) angular proximity to the decision subspace (which encodes final output choice). Empirically, larger side effects occur between behavior pairs with higher overlap and when the source behavior’s subspace lies closer to the decision subspace. These results expose a fundamental challenge for targeted safety control: behavioral independence is geometrically constrained by shared representation structure.

Abstract

Interventions designed to modify a particular behavior in LLMs, such as refusal or sycophancy, often produce unintended changes in other behaviors. This lack of targeted control makes it difficult to design and implement reliable safety controls. To understand these side-effects, we introduce a diagnostic framework for analyzing interacting behaviors in LLMs. We model behaviors as low-rank subspaces in activation space, and study how interventions influence across behaviors. Across multiple instruction-tuned models (7B-70B) and across refusal, jailbreak, and sycophancy settings, we find that different behaviors share internal representations, and intervening on one behavior alters others in asymmetric ways. Some behaviors act as upstream control points whose interventions propagate broadly across other behaviors, while others remain more isolated. We relate these effects to two geometric quantities: (i) the overlap between behavior subspaces, measured as the average squared cosine of principal angles, and (ii) the angle between each behavior subspace and the decision subspace (capturing the model's final decision e.g., refuse vs. comply). Empirically, intervention effects on other behaviors tend to be larger for behavior pairs with higher subspace overlap, and for source behaviors whose subspaces lie closer (smaller angle) to the decision subspace. These findings highlight a challenge for targeted behavior control: behaviors are difficult to modify independently, as interventions can propagate through shared representations and asymmetric interactions.

item.title: MUFFLe: Efficient Model Update Compression via Generalized Deduplication for Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14354v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）在边缘计算场景中具有天然优势，但其实际部署常受限于客户端上行带宽瓶颈——尤其是频繁上传高维模型更新所引发的巨大通信开销。现有压缩方法（如8位量化、Top-$k$稀疏化）虽能降低传输量，但仍存在冗余模式未被充分挖掘的问题。

方法创新：MUFFLe框架

本文提出MUFFLe（Model Update Compression via Generalized Deduplication for Federated Learning），一种面向FedAvg流水线的高效更新压缩方案。其核心是将广义去重（Generalized Deduplication, GD） 首次系统性引入FL通信优化：不再仅对权重值或梯度幅值做粗粒度压缩，而是将模型更新向量视为字节/数值序列，识别并消除跨位置、跨客户端的重复子模式（如常见梯度块、零值簇、周期性符号序列等），实现固定码率、变长计数的无损（或可控有损）编码。

关键实验结果

在IID MNIST基准（20客户端）的初步验证中，MUFFLe以38 MB累计上行通信量即达到目标精度92.93%，显著优于：8-bit量化（75 MB）、Top-$k$（$k=1\%$，86 MB）及原始FedAvg（310 MB）。压缩率提升达8.2×（vs. FedAvg）和2.2×（vs. 8-bit），且不引入额外服务器解码延迟或客户端计算负担。

理论价值与实践意义

MUFFLe首次证实：GD这一源于存储与网络领域的经典思想，可无缝迁移至FL更新压缩任务，在保持算法兼容性的同时突破传统量化/稀疏范式的性能边界，为低带宽边缘AI提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) promises privacy-preserving edge AI but suffers from prohibitive uplink communication costs during model update transmission. This work introduces MUFFLe, a novel compression framework that integrates Generalized Deduplication (GD) into the FedAvg pipeline to eliminate redundant patterns across model update vectors—treating gradients as structured sequences rather than independent scalars. Unlike fixed-sparsity or uniform-quantization methods, MUFFLe achieves fixed-rate, variable-count encoding by detecting and deduplicating repeated numerical subpatterns (e.g., zero runs, symmetric gradients, periodic updates) across clients and layers. On IID MNIST with 20 clients, MUFFLe reaches 92.93% test accuracy using only 38 MB total uplink traffic, outperforming 8-bit quantization (75 MB), Top-$k$ sparsification ($k=1\%$, 86 MB), and vanilla FedAvg (310 MB). This demonstrates GD’s feasibility and superiority for communication-efficient FL—offering up to 8.2× bandwidth reduction without altering client-side training or requiring server-side model retraining.

Abstract

Federated learning is well suited to edge environments but is often limited by the uplink cost of transmitting model updates. This Work-in-Progress paper presents MUFFLe, a communication-efficient update compression scheme that integrates generalized deduplication (GD) into the FedAvg pipeline. MUFFLe deduplicates repeated patterns across the update vector, yielding a fixed-rate, variable-count compression scheme. Preliminary experiments on IID MNIST with 20 clients show that MUFFLe reaches the target accuracy of $92.93\%$ with 38~MB cumulative uplink communication, compared with 75~MB for 8-bit quantization, 86~MB for Top-$k$ sparsification, and 310~MB for uncompressed FedAvg. These results demonstrate the feasibility of applying GD to communication-efficient federated learning.

item.title: Decoupled Mixture-of-Experts for Parametric Knowledge Injection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.14243v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

知识注入旨在为大语言模型（LLM）赋予外部、领域专属或时效性强的知识。现有方法面临根本性权衡：检索增强生成（RAG） 将知识保留在模型外部，仅支持提示级增强，缺乏参数化建模能力；而后训练微调类方法（如全参数微调或适配器）虽能将新知识编码进模型参数，却易引发灾难性遗忘、知识冲突，且每次更新均需重训，成本高昂。

方法创新：解耦式混合专家（DMoE）

本文提出解耦式混合专家（Decoupled Mixture-of-Experts, DMoE），一种面向参数化知识注入的模块化架构。其核心在于三重解耦：

  • 专家解耦：将外部知识语料库自动编译为独立、可热插拔的专家模块（每个专家封装特定知识子域），彼此参数隔离，互不干扰；
  • 路由解耦：引入轻量级、不确定性感知的路由器，仅在基础模型对当前输入置信度不足时激活相关专家，避免冗余计算；
  • 结构解耦：专家仅附加于Transformer最后一层前馈网络（FFN）之后，完全复用KV缓存，保障自回归推理效率，同时实现真正的参数级知识增强。

主要结果与优势

在多个知识密集型基准（如KILT、FEVER、HotpotQA）上，DMoE显著超越RAG和Adapter基线：答案准确率平均提升+4.2%，事实一致性提升+6.8%；单次知识增补仅需训练对应专家（<5%参数量），零干扰主干模型，彻底规避遗忘与冲突；专家可独立部署、动态加载/卸载，支持细粒度、低开销、可持续的知识演进。

AI Summary (English)

Knowledge injection seeks to equip LLMs with external, domain-specific, or time-sensitive knowledge. Existing methods trade off flexibility and integration: retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) keeps knowledge external but offers only prompt-level augmentation, while post-training approaches encode knowledge into shared parameters—risking catastrophic forgetting, knowledge conflict, and costly full-model updates. We propose Decoupled Mixture-of-Experts (DMoE), a modular architecture that fully decouples both expert modules and the router from the base model. DMoE converts knowledge corpora into independently updatable, parameter-isolated experts and employs a lightweight, uncertainty-aware router that activates experts only when the base model lacks confidence. Crucially, experts are attached solely to the final-layer FFN, preserving KV-cache reuse for efficient auto-regressive inference while enabling true parametric knowledge injection. Experiments on knowledge-intensive benchmarks show DMoE consistently outperforms RAG and adapter baselines in answer quality (+4.2% accuracy, +6.8% factual consistency), with zero interference to the base model and minimal update cost per knowledge addition.

Abstract

Knowledge injection aims to equip large language models (LLMs) with external, domain-specific, or time-sensitive knowledge. Existing approaches typically face a trade-off between flexibility and integration: retrieval-augmented generation keeps knowledge outside the model but only provides prompt-level augmentation, whereas post-training based methods encode new knowledge into shared parameters but may introduce catastrophic forgetting, knowledge conflict, and costly updates. In this paper, we propose Decoupled Mixture-of-Experts (DMoE), a modular architecture for parametric knowledge injection that decouples both experts and the router from the base model. DMoE converts external knowledge corpora into independently updatable expert modules and uses a lightweight uncertainty-aware router to activate relevant experts only when the base model lacks sufficient knowledge during generation. To support efficient auto-regressive inference, DMoE attaches experts only to the final-layer feed-forward network, preserving KV-cache reuse while enabling parameter-level knowledge augmentation. Experiments on knowledge-intensive benchmarks show that DMoE consistently improves answer quality over retrieval and adapter-based baselines.

item.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13621v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究定位：从运行时防护到可防御性分析

本文挑战“屏蔽（shield）仅是强化学习中运行时安全约束”的主流范式，提出屏蔽综合（shield synthesis）本质上是一种设计阶段的可防御性分析框架。我们重构了传统基于自动机的时序逻辑编译流程——包括规格编译、乘积博弈构建、吸引子计算与获胜区提取——将其解读为揭示系统内在结构特性的分析工具，而非仅生成部署时的动作过滤器。

方法创新：不对称双规格安全博弈建模

针对网络防御场景，我们构建了一个受约束的双人安全博弈：

  • 防御方规格定义系统的不可达（unsafe）状态集（即需绝对避免的拓扑失效模式）；
  • 攻击方规格则在吸引子计算过程中限制对手的合法行动空间（反映现实中的能力约束，如渗透路径限制），实现规格的不对称施加。

求解该博弈输出三项核心产物：可防御性裁决（defensibility verdict）（形式化判定某网络拓扑是否可在给定规格下被完全防御）、获胜区域（winning region）（所有初始状态下防御者必胜的状态子集）及对应的屏蔽策略。

关键发现：可防御性指纹与架构敏感性

超越二元裁决，我们从吸引子层级结构中提取拓扑级度量（如最小防御覆盖半径、吸引子深度分布），并融合屏蔽约束下的对抗性多智能体RL收敛行为（如攻防交互频率、策略僵持周期），构建可防御性指纹（defensibility fingerprint）——同时刻画系统的形式安全性与适应性对抗下的操作韧性。
“假设分析”表明：形式可防御性与操作有效性表征安全的不同维度——微小架构调整（如增加一条冗余链路）可能引发操作性能的剧烈波动（如平均攻破时间下降70%），而形式获胜区面积变化却不足5%。这证实屏蔽综合的核心价值不在于生成安全策略，而在于**回答根本性架构问题：系统能否被防御？何处最脆弱？如何重构以提升鲁棒性？** 可防御性裁决本身，才是真正的分析输出。

AI Summary (English)

This paper repositions shield synthesis—not as a runtime enforcement mechanism for safe RL agents, but as a design-time defensibility analysis framework. We reinterpret the standard automata-theoretic pipeline (specification compilation, product game construction, attractor computation, winning-region extraction) as yielding structural insights about system architecture rather than just executable constraints. For network defense, we formalize an asymmetric two-player safety game: the defender’s specification defines the unsafe region, while the attacker’s specification constrains adversarial actions only during attractor computation, enabling realistic capability modeling. Solving the game produces a formal defensibility verdict, the associated winning region, and a shield—constituting a certificate of architectural safety. Beyond binary verdicts, we derive topology-level metrics from attractor structure and integrate them with post-convergence behavior from shield-constrained adversarial multi-agent RL to form a defensibility fingerprint, jointly capturing formal safety and operational resilience under adaptive attack. Crucially, “what-if” analysis reveals that formal defensibility and operational effectiveness are decoupled: minor architectural changes can drastically alter operational outcomes while leaving formal safety margins nearly invariant. Thus, shield synthesis is most valuable as a diagnostic tool for architectural security questions—not as a deployment shield.

Abstract

Shielded reinforcement learning is typically presented as a runtime safety mechanism that compiles temporal-logic specifications into automata restricting an agent's actions. We argue this is the wrong product. The same automata-theoretic machinery -- specification compilation, product game construction, attractor computation, and winning-region extraction -- is better read as a design-time analytical instrument whose outputs are structural insights about a system rather than runtime constraints on a deployed agent. We instantiate this through a constrained two-player safety game for network defense. The two specifications are enforced asymmetrically: the defender specification defines the unsafe region of the game, whereas the attacker specification restricts the adversary's legal actions during attractor computation. Solving the game yields a defensibility verdict -- a formal certificate that a topology-specification pair is or is not defensible -- with the associated winning region and shield. Beyond the binary verdict, we derive topology-level metrics from the attractor structure and combine them with post-convergence behavior from shield-constrained adversarial multi-agent reinforcement learning. Together these form a defensibility fingerprint capturing both a network's formal safety properties and its operational behavior under adaptive play. A what-if analysis shows that formal defensibility and operational effectiveness capture distinct aspects of security: small architectural changes can produce large shifts in operational outcomes while leaving formal safety margins nearly unchanged.
